strategies.name: Generalist Vote 15
strategies.description: Fifteen independent generalists followed by deterministic plurality.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and return the single best complete answer for every case.

strategies.name: Diversified Vote 15
strategies.description: Fifteen independent solvers with varied mechanism lenses, then deterministic plurality.

stages.instruction: Solve independently through the assigned lens, but search other allowed families if that lens fails.

strategies.name: E04 Verified Gate
strategies.description: The Experiment 04 champion architecture: seven proposers, two verifiers, and one judge with a unanimity gate.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and justify one complete allowed mechanism.
Reconstruct the strongest candidates independently and report the answer that survives complete replay.
Choose one exact candidate or a fully checked replacement. Do not average competing tuples.

strategies.name: E04 Proposer Pool
strategies.description: The seven-proposer pool from the Experiment 04 champion with the decision layer removed.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and justify one complete allowed mechanism.

strategies.name: Three-Judge Verified Gate
strategies.description: Nine proposers, three independent verifiers, and three judges with a two-judge, two-verifier override gate.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and return one complete full-prefix answer.
Reconstruct and stress-test the leading and coherent minority candidates.
Make an independent evidence-based decision without inventing a compromise tuple.

strategies.name: Critic-Supported Dual Judge
strategies.description: Ten proposers, three falsifying critics, and two judges. Both judges and one critic must support an override.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and give the simplest complete mechanism.
Try to falsify the proposals and repair them only with a complete allowed mechanism.
Choose the answer with the strongest complete-prefix support.

strategies.name: Verifier-Heavy Jury
strategies.description: Eight proposers, four verifiers, and three judges with a stringent verifier-backed override.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and report one exact answer.
Independently reconstruct the best candidates and check every visible term.
Choose one fully supported answer and reject persuasive partial fits.

strategies.name: Large Pool Conservative Gate
strategies.description: Twelve proposers, two verifiers, and one judge. The judge may override only with unanimous verifier support.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and require a full-prefix fit.
Reconstruct the leading candidates and report the one that survives exact replay.
Select the best fully checked answer.

strategies.name: Critic Verifier Hybrid
strategies.description: Eight diverse proposers, three critics, two verifiers, and two judges with mixed independent support.

stages.instruction: Solve independently through the assigned lens.
Falsify weak candidates and identify the strongest complete alternative.
Reconstruct the strongest surviving mechanisms from scratch.
Select one exact answer using complete-prefix evidence.

strategies.name: Alternative-Hypothesis Tribunal
strategies.description: A small proposer pool is challenged by four alternate-hypothesis critics, then checked by verifiers and dual judges.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and state one complete mechanism.
Construct and test a materially different allowed explanation, then report the best-supported answer.
Replay the strongest competing mechanisms exactly.
Choose the answer that survives the strongest competing explanation.

strategies.name: Audited Integrator
strategies.description: Ten proposers, two critics, two judges, and one final integrator. The integrator needs two upstream supporters to override.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and require a complete allowed mechanism.
Identify concrete failures and the best repaired answer.
Choose one exact answer independently.
Audit the full evidence and return the single answer that best survives complete replay.

strategies.name: Judge-Free Weighted Review
strategies.description: Seven proposers, four critics, and four verifiers. A weighted deterministic vote replaces the judge bottleneck.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and return one exact answer.
Falsify weak proposals and report the strongest complete answer.
Independently replay and verify the strongest candidates.

strategies.name: Arithmetic Error Firewall
strategies.description: Eight proposers feed four arithmetic auditors and a three-member jury.

stages.instruction: Infer a complete mechanism and calculate the next five terms.
Audit indexing, signs, recurrence arithmetic, and every visible transition. Report the corrected exact answer.
Choose the answer with a reproducible mechanism and clean arithmetic.

strategies.name: Ambiguity Breaker
strategies.description: Eight proposers are opposed by four explicit alternative-mechanism challengers before a three-judge gate.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and state the strongest complete mechanism.
Search for a different allowed family that fits the whole prefix, and compare its prediction exactly.
Resolve the competing mechanisms through full-prefix evidence.

strategies.name: No-Judge Conservative Challenge
strategies.description: Twelve proposers establish a strong baseline. Three challengers can override only when at least two independently agree.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and require a full-prefix mechanism.
Try to disprove the plurality and report a replacement only when fully reconstructed.

strategies.name: Dispute-Only Escalation
strategies.description: Nine proposers solve every case. Critics and verifiers spend calls only on cases where the proposer pool disagrees.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and return one exact answer.
Focus only on disputed cases. Falsify candidates and report the strongest complete answer.
For the remaining disputed cases, reconstruct the strongest candidates exactly.

strategies.name: Two-Branch Cross Examination
strategies.description: Two independent five-solver branches are cross-examined, then two integrators must agree with upstream support.

stages.instruction: Solve independently from scratch.
Solve independently from scratch without relying on another branch.
Cross-examine the competing branches and identify concrete failures or a surviving answer.
Independently select the exact answer that survives cross-examination.

strategies.name: Hypothesis Tournament
strategies.description: Six proposals enter a critic and verifier tournament before two independent judges decide.

stages.instruction: Generate and test the best complete hypothesis.
Eliminate hypotheses that fail any visible term and report the strongest survivor.
Independently reproduce the strongest surviving mechanism.
Choose the exact answer that survives the tournament.

strategies.name: Adaptive Dispute Jury
strategies.description: Eight solvers handle all cases. A seven-member jury is invoked only where those solvers disagree.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and report one exact answer.
For disputed cases only, independently select or reconstruct the best-supported answer.

strategies.name: Proof-Carrying Consensus
strategies.description: Ten independent proposals are followed by five proof auditors. Three auditors must agree to change the base plurality.

stages.instruction: Solve independently and provide a concise reproducible rule.
Accept an answer only if its mechanism can be reconstructed and replayed across the full prefix.
